@@ -224,7 +224,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.1","title":"T6.7.9.1 Deterministic physical-writer batching lane and ordering semantics","description":"Implement the explicit physical-writer batching lane for WAL append/fsync work. This subtask is about deterministic batch composition, commit ordering, and observability of the physical writer as a first-class runtime component rather than an incidental serialized tail step.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The runtime has an explicit physical-writer batching lane with deterministic batch ordering and membership rules.
2. Unit and deterministic integration tests cover commit ordering, first failure propagation, interaction with logical commit preparation, and fairness-budget enforcement.
3. The batching lane has explicit max-wait/max-size or equivalent fairness controls, plus an operator-visible rollback/disable path, so low-latency writers do not suffer unbounded queueing in pursuit of throughput.
4. Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_physical_writer_lane.sh` to exercise deterministic file-backed grouped-write workloads, fairness budgets, and replayable batch artifacts.
5. Structured logs emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `batch_id`, `batch_membership`, `queue_delay_ns`, `target_wait_ns`, `max_wait_ns`, `fsync_boundary`, `ordering_phase`, `rollback_mode_active`, and failure context.
6. The batching lane preserves durability and FCW/visibility semantics rather than smearing them across convenience abstractions.","notes":"User-facing payoff:
- Multi-writer workloads should spend less wall-clock time queued behind physical write completion.
- This bead explicitly guards against throughput wins that come from making unlucky writers wait too long.

Verification blueprint:
- Unit/property layer:
  - Cover ordering semantics, first-failure propagation, fairness budgets, and rollback/disable behavior.
- E2E/replay layer:
  - `scripts/verify_t6_7_physical_writer_lane.sh` must emit replay commands, artifact hashes, and bounded-wait evidence for deterministic file-backed workloads.
- Logging contract:
  - A human should be able to reconstruct why a batch formed and whether a fairness budget or rollback gate constrained it.

Planning intent:
- This is the baseline lane that enables `bd-1dp9.6.7.9.4`.
- Do not confuse deterministic batching mechanics with the final service design; the promised-land target is an explainable bounded-latency policy, not just a queue that happens to batch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:09.196830104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T07:51:27.592987185Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T07:51:27.592987185Z","close_reason":"Implemented physical writer lane verification and observability contract","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","checkpoint","group-commit","optimization","performance","track6","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.2","title":"T6.7.9.2 Checkpoint decoupling and foreground-path isolation","description":"Separate checkpoint interaction from the foreground physical writer path so checkpoint work cannot quietly dominate the same latency-critical lane under a new label. This subtask owns the explicit choreography between grouped commit and checkpoint progress.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Foreground grouped commit and checkpoint work have explicit interaction rules and no longer share an accidental choke point.
2. Deterministic tests cover checkpoint-triggered edge cases, interference control, and failure behavior.
3. Structured traces expose checkpoint/foreground overlap, stalls avoided, and any remaining synchronized phases.
4. The resulting design preserves crash/recovery semantics and user-visible durability guarantees.","notes":"Planning note:
- This should follow the batching-lane implementation because the point is to keep checkpoint work from collapsing back into that lane.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:09.943310793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T08:09:00.255196220Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T08:09:00.255196220Z","close_reason":"Implemented checkpoint/foreground coordination tracing, deterministic tests, and verification script","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","checkpoint","group-commit","optimization","performance","track6","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.3","title":"T6.7.9.3 Group-commit durability, fairness, and failure-injection evidence pack","description":"Publish the proof bundle for the grouped physical writer and checkpoint decoupling work: deterministic multi-writer e2e scenarios, crash/failure injection, fairness measurements, and richly structured batch/fsync/checkpoint logs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. End-to-end scripts cover grouped commit ordering, checkpoint interaction, crash/restart recovery, and multi-writer fairness on file-backed storage.
2. Structured logs include batch IDs, fsync boundaries, checkpoint phase markers, durability outcomes, and first-failure diagnostics.
3. Replay instructions and artifact hashes are published and suitable for future regression guarding.
4. Evidence demonstrates user-relevant throughput/fairness improvement without durability or correctness drift.","notes":"Verification blueprint:
- Unit/property layer:
  - Cover grouped commit ordering, batch membership, first failure propagation, checkpoint interaction, and crash/restart durability semantics.
  - Include queue-delay and fairness-budget checks so batching cannot silently inflate tail latency.
- E2E/replay layer:
  - Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_group_commit.sh` for multi-writer grouped-commit workloads, checkpoint interference tests, crash/restart replay, durability/fairness measurement, and bounded-batch-wait validation.
- Logging contract:
  - Emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `batch_id`, `batch_size`, `queue_delay_ns`, `fsync_boundary`, `checkpoint_phase`, `commit_order`, `durability_outcome`, and first-failure diagnostics.
- User-facing intent:
  - Batching should reduce queueing pain for writers without hiding durability or worsening tail latency for unlucky sessions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:10.666335522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:46:03.479745813Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","checkpoint","group-commit","optimization","performance","track6","verification","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.4","title":"T6.7.9.4 Bounded-latency commit service policy with adaptive fairness control","description":"Add an explicit bounded-latency commit service policy above the physical writer lane. The deterministic group-commit path is the baseline; this bead makes it next-gen by introducing an evidence-backed control policy that adapts batch wait, batch size, or flush cadence to observed load while still enforcing explicit fairness and latency budgets.

This is not permission for opaque heuristics. The service policy must be simple enough to reason about, measurable enough to tune, and strict enough that throughput gains cannot come from quietly sacrificing unlucky writers.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The physical writer exposes an explicit service policy with user-relevant fairness/latency budgets and adaptive controls for batch formation under changing load.
2. Deterministic unit/property tests cover controller stability/hysteresis, budget compliance, starvation prevention, and behavior under bursty, sparse, and mixed-duration writer populations.
3. Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_commit_service_policy.sh` to run bursty multi-writer, long/short transaction mix, and checkpoint-interference scenarios with fixed seeds and replayable artifacts.
4. Structured logs emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `control_epoch`, `target_wait_ns`, `actual_wait_ns`, `queue_age_p95_ns`, `batch_size`, `fairness_budget_ns`, `starvation_prevented`, `mode_switch_reason`, and first-failure diagnostics.
5. The policy improves throughput/fairness tradeoffs without regressing durability, FCW/SSI correctness, or concurrent-mode default behavior.","notes":"Future-self intent:
- This bead is where we allow disciplined control-theoretic thinking into the write path.
- Keep the controller explainable. If a human cannot understand why the system waited, flushed, or changed mode from the logs, the design is not done.
- User-facing payoff: better p95/p99 write experience under load, not just prettier average throughput numbers.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-06T06:45:58.568077048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:47:40.687018287Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-uplift","architecture-audit-2026-03","checkpoint","group-commit","next-gen-architecture","optimization","performance","promised-land","track6","verification","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.4","title":"T6.7.9.4 Bounded-latency commit service policy with adaptive fairness control","description":"Add an explicit bounded-latency commit service policy above the physical writer lane. The deterministic group-commit path is the baseline; this bead makes it next-gen by introducing an evidence-backed control policy that adapts batch wait, batch size, or flush cadence to observed load while still enforcing explicit fairness and latency budgets.

This is not permission for opaque heuristics. The service policy must be simple enough to reason about, measurable enough to tune, and strict enough that throughput gains cannot come from quietly sacrificing unlucky writers.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The physical writer exposes an explicit service policy with user-relevant fairness/latency budgets and adaptive controls for batch formation under changing load.
2. Deterministic unit/property tests cover controller stability/hysteresis, budget compliance, starvation prevention, and behavior under bursty, sparse, and mixed-duration writer populations.
3. Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_commit_service_policy.sh` to run bursty multi-writer, long/short transaction mix, and checkpoint-interference scenarios with fixed seeds and replayable artifacts.
4. Structured logs emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `control_epoch`, `target_wait_ns`, `actual_wait_ns`, `queue_age_p95_ns`, `batch_size`, `fairness_budget_ns`, `starvation_prevented`, `mode_switch_reason`, and first-failure diagnostics.
5. The policy improves throughput/fairness tradeoffs without regressing durability, FCW/SSI correctness, or concurrent-mode default behavior.","notes":"Future-self intent:
- This bead is where we allow disciplined control-theoretic thinking into the write path.
- Keep the controller explainable. If a human cannot understand why the system waited, flushed, or changed mode from the logs, the design is not done.
- User-facing payoff: better p95/p99 write experience under load, not just prettier average throughput numbers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-03-06T06:45:58.568077048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T08:37:36Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T08:37:36Z","close_reason":"Implemented bounded-latency commit service policy, verifier script, and structured control telemetry","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-uplift","architecture-audit-2026-03","checkpoint","group-commit","next-gen-architecture","optimization","performance","promised-land","track6","verification","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.7","title":"Track 7: Verification Stack, Logging, and CI Gates","description":"## Context
We need test realism, exhaustive coverage, and actionable logs. Existing hardening work must be integrated, not duplicated.

## Goal
Build comprehensive deterministic unit/e2e/differential verification with rich structured logging and CI enforcement.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit and E2E matrices are scenario-complete for parity scope.
- Logs are schema-governed and triage-ready.
- CI gates prevent drift and provide machine-readable evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:23.712070243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:42:47.098356142Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:42:47.098290078Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","e2e","logging","parity100","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":262,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track 7 operating notes:
- This track is the mechanical enforcement layer: comprehensive tests, logging contracts, CI publication, replay UX.
- It is the primary defense against silent parity regression.

Design choices:
- Unit matrix keyed to taxonomy IDs (Track 1).
- E2E script suite must be scenario-complete with consistent event schema.
- CI must always publish enough artifacts for one-command reproduction.
- Replay tooling should get operators to minimal reproducer quickly.

Test/logging requirements:
- Deterministic unit suite expansion with gap coverage accounting.
- E2E scripts produce structured logs with run_id, seed, phase transitions, divergence markers.
- CI logs include flake budget accounting and auto-bisect metadata.

Definition of done:
- Any failing parity gate yields actionable artifact bundle and fast reproduction path.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:11:25Z"},{"id":263,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"All 7 child tasks CLOSED. Track 7 deliverables complete: T7.1 deterministic unit matrix, T7.2 scenario-complete E2E suite, T7.3 CI matrix with flake budgets and auto-bisect, T7.4 failure replay/triage harness, T7.5 verification contract gate, T7.6 log schema validator and replay decoder, T7.7 contract enforcement integration. Closing epic.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:42:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.7.1","title":"T7.1 Deterministic unit matrix expansion mapped to parity taxonomy","description":"Expand unit tests for every taxonomy bucket with deterministic seed strategy and failure diagnostics contract. Acceptance: each bucket has explicit unit coverage and invariant assertions.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:33.149208890Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T05:06:29.475125204Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T05:06:29.475106359Z","close_reason":"Complete: 41 test entries across 9 taxonomy categories with deterministic seeds, 13 unit tests, E2E script, structured JSON logging","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["invariants","parity100","unit-tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":264,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.7.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Mandatory Verification Contract (applies to this bead)

This task is not complete until evidence is attached for all three layers:
1. **Unit tests**: deterministic tests proving local semantics/invariants for the changed surface.
2. **E2E scripts**: scenario-level scripts exercising realistic flows and differential behavior where applicable.
3. **Structured logging artifacts**: machine-parseable logs with run_id, seed/config, phase markers, first-divergence indicators (when relevant), and artifact hashes.

Minimum evidence bundle:
- test identifiers + pass/fail summary,
- reproduction command(s),
- output artifact locations/hashes,
- short rationale linking evidence to acceptance criteria.

Gate policy:
- Missing any one of the three layers = bead remains open.
- Evidence must be reproducible on a clean checkout with pinned config/seeds.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:32Z"},{"id":265,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.7.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Evidence: 13 unit tests passing, 41 test entries across 9 taxonomy categories, 2 property-based tests, E2E script (scripts/verify_unit_matrix.sh --json), deterministic seeds via xxh3. Reproduction: cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib -- unit_matrix && bash scripts/verify_unit_matrix.sh --json","created_at":"2026-02-13T05:06:24Z"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+BEAD_ID="bd-1dp9.6.7.9.4"
+RUN_ID="${BEAD_ID}-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
+ARTIFACT_DIR="artifacts/${BEAD_ID}/${RUN_ID}"
+TARGET_DIR="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_bd_1dp9_6_7_9_4"
+POLICY_LOG="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/commit_service_policy_tests.log"
+CHECKPOINT_LOG="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/checkpoint_coordination_tests.log"
+SHA_LOG="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/sha256.txt"
+REPORT_JSON="${ARTIFACT_DIR}/gate_report.json"
+
+mkdir -p "${ARTIFACT_DIR}"
+
+policy_cmd=(
+  rch exec -- env "CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TARGET_DIR}"
+  cargo test -p fsqlite-pager commit_service_policy_ -- --nocapture
+)
+checkpoint_cmd=(
+  rch exec -- env "CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TARGET_DIR}"
+  cargo test -p fsqlite-pager checkpoint_coordination -- --nocapture
+)
+
+printf '+'
+printf ' %q' "${policy_cmd[@]}"
+printf '\n'
+"${policy_cmd[@]}" 2>&1 | tee "${POLICY_LOG}"
+
+printf '+'
+printf ' %q' "${checkpoint_cmd[@]}"
+printf '\n'
+"${checkpoint_cmd[@]}" 2>&1 | tee "${CHECKPOINT_LOG}"
+
+rg -q 'control_epoch=' "${POLICY_LOG}"
+rg -q 'target_wait_ns=' "${POLICY_LOG}"
+rg -q 'actual_wait_ns=' "${POLICY_LOG}"
+rg -q 'queue_age_p95_ns=' "${POLICY_LOG}"
+rg -q 'batch_size=' "${POLICY_LOG}"
+rg -q 'fairness_budget_ns=' "${POLICY_LOG}"
+rg -q 'mode_switch_reason=' "${POLICY_LOG}"
+rg -q 'mode_switch_reason="sparse_queue".*service_policy_mode="low_latency"|service_policy_mode="low_latency".*mode_switch_reason="sparse_queue"' "${POLICY_LOG}"
+rg -q 'mode_switch_reason="sparse_queue".*starvation_prevented=false|starvation_prevented=false.*mode_switch_reason="sparse_queue"' "${POLICY_LOG}"
+rg -q 'mode_switch_reason="tail_latency_pressure".*service_policy_mode="low_latency"|service_policy_mode="low_latency".*mode_switch_reason="tail_latency_pressure"' "${POLICY_LOG}"
+rg -q 'mode_switch_reason="tail_latency_pressure".*starvation_prevented=true|starvation_prevented=true.*mode_switch_reason="tail_latency_pressure"' "${POLICY_LOG}"
+rg -q 'checkpoint_phase="active_gate".*foreground_phase="begin".*interaction_rule="checkpoint_excludes_new_transactions"' "${CHECKPOINT_LOG}"
+rg -q 'checkpoint_phase="backend_owned".*foreground_phase="foreground_idle".*foreground_action="checkpoint_runs"' "${CHECKPOINT_LOG}"
+
+sha256sum "${POLICY_LOG}" "${CHECKPOINT_LOG}" | tee "${SHA_LOG}" >/dev/null
+
+jq -n \
+  --arg bead_id "${BEAD_ID}" \
+  --arg run_id "${RUN_ID}" \
+  --arg policy_log "${POLICY_LOG}" \
+  --arg checkpoint_log "${CHECKPOINT_LOG}" \
+  --arg policy_cmd "${policy_cmd[*]}" \
+  --arg checkpoint_cmd "${checkpoint_cmd[*]}" \
+  --arg sha_log "${SHA_LOG}" \
+  '{
+    bead_id: $bead_id,
+    run_id: $run_id,
+    policy_log: $policy_log,
+    checkpoint_log: $checkpoint_log,
+    replay_commands: {
+      commit_service_policy: $policy_cmd,
+      checkpoint_coordination: $checkpoint_cmd
+    },
+    artifacts: {
+      sha256: $sha_log
+    },
+    assertions: [
+      "service-policy traces emit control epoch, bounded waits, queue-age p95, fairness budget, batch size, and switch reasons",
+      "sparse queue traffic stays in low-latency mode without tripping starvation prevention",
+      "tail-latency pressure flips the controller into starvation prevention with zero additional wait",
+      "checkpoint coordination still excludes new foreground transactions while the service policy is active",
+      "checkpoint ownership and foreground-idle transitions remain visible in structured logs"
+    ]
+  }' > "${REPORT_JSON}"
+
+echo "[GATE PASS] ${BEAD_ID} commit service policy verification passed"
+echo "Artifacts: ${ARTIFACT_DIR}"
